methodology: Five real component measures. Each raw value (sourced, dated) is mapped to 0–10 by a fixed published range (see ranges). Composite = equal-weight mean of available components. No estimates; a country missing a source shows that component dark.
data_status: real — fetched from World Bank API + OWID; values normalized to 0–10 by published ranges; gaps omitted

countries.name: Ukraine
countries.note: active conflict; civic gains under wartime conditions

countries.name: Israel
countries.note: score reflects citizens within Israel; OPT not included

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.note: women's labor force expansion driving recent gains

countries.name: Afghanistan
countries.note: women's freedoms and education collapse since 2021

countries.name: India
countries.note: rapid material gains; civic indicators softer

countries.name: Bangladesh
countries.note: child mortality and female literacy gains widely cited

countries.name: China
countries.note: 800M+ lifted from extreme poverty since 1990; civic indicators decline

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.note: poverty rate from 70% in 1990 to under 5%

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.note: child mortality halved since 2000; Tigray a recent setback

countries.name: Rwanda
countries.note: post-genocide reconstruction model on health and admin; civic indicators contested

countries.name: Cuba
countries.note: healthspan strong; civic indicators severely constrained

access.unit: % (avg of internet, electricity, safe water, literacy)
